plan_metadata.name: Sample Graph Connector App Local Debug

steps.description: Click the "Search" input box within the "Samples" tab of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Custom Engine Agent" option in the "Platform capability..." dropdown menu within the Samples workspace of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Create" button for the "Copilot connector" sample in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension tab within Visual Studio Code.
And input ${{var:copilot-connector-app}} into the run time variable.
Click the "Run and Debug (Ctrl+Shift+D)" button in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel of Visual Studio Code's sidebar to initiate debugging actions for the copilot-connector-app.
Click the green "Start Debugging" button in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code to initiate debugging.
Click the "Debug Teams (Chrome)" option in the debug configuration dropdown of Visual Studio Code's Run and Debug panel.
Click the "Debug" dropdown in the Run and Debug panel header on Visual Studio Code’s left sidebar.
Click the "Sign in to Azure" link under the "test01@xxbdw.onmicrosoft.com" account in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug in Docker (Edge)" dropdown button in the Run and Debug panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug in Docker (Chrome)" option in the Debug configuration dropdown menu within the Run and Debug sidebar of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Debug" drop-down menu in the "RUN AND DEBUG" panel at the top left of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Show user profile using Single Sign On feature" card in the SSO Bot local tab of Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Send" button (paper plane icon) in the message input area to send the typed message "show" in the Microsoft Teams SSO Bot local chat window.
